name: Boavista Trail (Yellow)
name_original: Percurso Boavista (Amarelo) na Tapada de Mafra
description: Jog the moderate 4.9-mile Boavista Trail (Yellow) in Tapada de Mafra, gaining 598 feet of elevation through diverse forests and past a histo
region_intro: Jogging around Tapada Nacional de Mafra offers an immersive experience within a historic former royal hunting ground, now a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The region is characterized by extensive and diverse forests, featuring species like maritime pines, cork oaks, and Portuguese oaks, enveloped by a 21 km wall. Joggers will find varied terrain with trails offering different distances and elevation changes, from gentle paths along streams to more challenging forest runs. This natural environment provides opportunities for wildlife spotting, including deer and wild boar, far from urban noise. Best jogging routes around Tapada Nacional de Mafra The most popular jogging route is Tojeira (Green) Trail, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete. This moderate route passes through diverse forests and pastures, offering a varied landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Taipas Trail (Red) in the National Tapada of Mafra, a moderate 5.2 miles (8.3 km) path. This route features a mix of forest paths and open areas, with an elevation gain of 473 feet (144 meters). Local runners also love the Ribeirinho (Blue) Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through shaded areas along the Safarujo stream, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around Tapada Nacional de Mafra is defined by dense forests, varied terrain, and opportunities for wildlife observation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging forest trails. The routes in Tapada Nacional de Mafra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 90 reviews. More than 3900 runners have used komoot to explore Tapada Nacional de Mafra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: Tapada Nacional de Mafra offers a wide selection of over 90 running routes. These trails cater to various preferences, from easy paths to more challenging forest runs, ensuring a suitable option for every jogger.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: The terrain in Tapada Nacional de Mafra is quite varied, featuring extensive and diverse forests with species like maritime pines, cork oaks, and Portuguese oaks. You'll find a mix of well-maintained trails, forest paths, and open areas, with varying distances and elevation changes. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381154903/ribeirinho-blue-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeirinho (Blue) Trail&lt;/a&gt;, offer shaded paths along streams, while others, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381155969/tojeira-green-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tojeira (Green) Trail&lt;/a&gt;, pass through diverse forests and pastures.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Tapada Nacional de Mafra has several easy running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381154903/ribeirinho-blue-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeirinho (Blue) Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 2.7-mile (4.4 km) path that leads through shaded areas along the Safarujo stream, perfect for a calmer run or walk. Overall, there are 7 easy routes available, providing gentle options for all.

Q: Can I spot any wildlife while jogging in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: Absolutely! Tapada Nacional de Mafra is renowned for its rich fauna. Joggers frequently have the opportunity to see fallow deer, red deer, and wild boar roaming freely, especially in areas like the pastures during the evening. The park is also home to foxes, rabbits, and over seventy species of birds, offering a unique connection with nature away from city noise.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in the park?
A: Many of the trails within Tapada Nacional de Mafra are suitable for families, particularly the easier and moderate routes. The park's peaceful environment and varied landscapes make it an enjoyable place for families to jog together. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381154903/ribeirinho-blue-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeirinho (Blue) Trail&lt;/a&gt;, with its gentle terrain and shaded areas, is a great option for families.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: The running routes in Tapada Nacional de Mafra are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 90 reviews. Reviewers often praise the serene and refreshing atmosphere, the diverse forest landscapes, and the opportunities for wildlife observation. The well-defined trails and varied difficulty levels also contribute to a highly enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or scenic viewpoints along the running trails?
A: Yes, the park offers several points of interest. You can explore the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302068" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapada Nacional de Mafra&lt;/a&gt; itself, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Other highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6302066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lime Kiln of Tapada de Mafra&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7195697" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Portuguese Combatant&lt;/a&gt;. The 'Enchanted Forest' within the park also provides particularly captivating views, especially from higher points.

Q: Is Tapada Nacional de Mafra accessible by public transport for runners?
A: While the region is primarily accessed by car, there are public transport options to Mafra town. From there, local transport or a short taxi ride would be needed to reach the entrance of Tapada Nacional de Mafra. It's advisable to check local bus schedules in advance for the most up-to-date information.

Q: What are the parking facilities like for joggers at Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: Tapada Nacional de Mafra provides designated parking areas for visitors. These are typically located near the main entrance or specific trailheads, making it convenient for runners to access the routes. It's recommended to arrive early during peak times to secure a spot.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: The policy regarding dogs in Tapada Nacional de Mafra can vary and is subject to park regulations, especially due to the presence of wildlife. It's best to check the official park website or contact them directly for the most current information on their dog policy before planning your run with a pet.

Q: What is the best season for running in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
A: Tapada Nacional de Mafra offers a pleasant running experience throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery with changing foliage. Summer mornings and evenings are also popular, offering cooler conditions. Winter runs can be refreshing, but some trails might be muddier after rain. The extensive tree cover provides shade, making it enjoyable even on warmer days.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Tapada Nacional de Mafra are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point without retracing your steps. This includes popular options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381148434/boavista-trail-yellow" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boavista Trail (Yellow)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1381157277/taipas-trail-red-in-the-national-tapada-of-mafra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taipas Trail (Red)&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied loops through the park's diverse landscapes.
tips: The Tapada Nacional de Mafra, created during the reign of João V in Portugal, is a royal park measuring more than 8 km², home to several species of animals and a diverse natural habitat. Used for hunting and leisure by the Portuguese monarchy, today it offers activities such as hiking, cycling, horse riding and archery. In 2019, it was classified as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
Old lime kiln in the center of Tapada de Mafra. It was built with the purpose of producing lime used to paint the external wall of the Tapada.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Boavista Trail (Yellow) for jogging?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered moderate in difficulty for jogging. While it's approximately 8 kilometers long, it features more elevation than some of the easier routes in Tapada Nacional de Mafra, including a significant uphill section. It's best suited for those comfortable with varied, natural surfaces and inclines, rather than beginners looking for a flat, easy run.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the Boavista Trail (Yellow)?
faqs.answer: The trail features diverse and natural terrain. You'll encounter dirt paths and potentially uneven ground, as it winds through areas of mixed forest, pine forest, scrubland, and pastures. Due to its varied topography, it is not recommended for prams or strollers.

faqs.question: Is the Boavista Trail (Yellow) a loop, and where does it start?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Boavista Trail (Yellow) is a circular route. It starts and ends within the Tapada Nacional de Mafra. Specific trailhead access points are typically well-marked within the park, often near the main entrance or designated parking areas.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I see while jogging this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers excellent opportunities for wildlife observation. As it passes through the protected Tapada Nacional de Mafra, you might spot fallow deer, deer, wild boar, and various birds of prey. Encountering deer is a common experience along this route.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Boavista Trail (Yellow)?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in Tapada Nacional de Mafra can vary. It's always best to check the official park website or signage at the entrance for the most current rules on whether dogs are permitted and if they need to be on a leash.

faqs.question: Do I need to pay an entrance fee or get a permit to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Boavista Trail (Yellow) is located within the Tapada Nacional de Mafra, which is a protected natural reserve. There is typically an entrance fee to access the park. You do not need a separate permit specifically for the Boavista Trail, but the park entrance fee covers access to its trails. For current fees and opening hours, please consult the official Tapada Nacional de Mafra website.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The Boavista Trail (Yellow) can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer mornings or late afternoons can also be good, but be mindful of heat. Winter can be beautiful, but check weather conditions for rain or mud, as the natural surfaces can become slippery.

faqs.question: What notable features or landmarks will I encounter?
faqs.answer: Beyond the natural beauty of the Tapada Nacional de Mafra itself, you'll pass through areas of mixed and pine forest, scrubland, and pastures. The trail crosses some of the highest points of the forest, offering beautiful views. You will also pass by the Lime Kiln of Tapada de Mafra, a historical man-made monument.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes in Tapada Nacional de Mafra?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Boavista Trail (Yellow), also known as Percurso Boavista (amarelo), is part of a network of trails within the park. While you'll primarily be on the yellow route, sections of it may intersect or run concurrently with other official trails such as Percurso Ribeirinho (azul), Percurso Taipas (vermelho), and Percurso Tojeira (verde).

faqs.question: Is there parking available for the Boavista Trail (Yellow)?
faqs.answer: Yes, parking is available at the main entrance of the Tapada Nacional de Mafra. From there, you can access the various trails, including the Boavista Trail (Yellow). It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends or holidays, as parking can fill up.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to wear appropriate trail running shoes. Bring sufficient water, especially on warmer days, as there are no water sources directly on the trail. A small backpack with snacks, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone are also good ideas. Don't forget binoculars if you're keen on spotting wildlife!

waypoints.name: Lime Kiln of Tapada de Mafra
